Component Reference

Reusable UI patterns for HTML mockup prototyping. Copy the markup and CSS classes into your mockup.

Toggle Group / Segmented Control

Grouped buttons where one is active at a time. Use for view mode switches, type filters, or any mutually exclusive options.

Icon toggle
Text toggle

Switch / Toggle

Boolean on/off toggle with sliding dot. Use for publish states, feature flags, or any binary setting.

Published
Notifications

Search Input with Clear

Input with search icon on the left and conditional clear button on the right. Icon uses absolute positioning; input has left padding to avoid overlap.

Tag Input

Editable tag list with per-tag removal and add input. Tags display as badges with an X button; new tags are added via text input + Enter or button.

No tags

Modal Dialog

Two variants: destructive confirmation and form dialog. Both use a fixed overlay with centered panel. Close on backdrop click or Cancel.

Delete Item?

This will permanently delete this item. This action cannot be undone.

Edit Item

Update item metadata.

Collapsible Section

Expandable content with chevron indicator. Uses its own x-data scope so it's self-contained — drop it anywhere without wiring up parent state.

This content is hidden by default and revealed when the header is clicked. The chevron rotates 180° to indicate the expanded state.
This section starts expanded. Set expanded: true in the initial x-data to control the default state.

Empty State

Conditional messaging when a list has no items. Shows different copy depending on whether filters are active (helping the user understand why the list is empty).

List has items — empty state is hidden.

Select Dropdown

Custom select built with Alpine.js for consistent styling across browsers. Uses @click.outside to close. The options list renders as an absolute-positioned div — the parent container must not have overflow-hidden.

Dropdown Menu

Menu triggered by a 3-dot (more) button. Uses its own x-data scope and closes on outside click via @click.outside.

Item row example

Tooltip

Info tooltip on hover using @mouseenter / @mouseleave. Uses its own x-data scope. Parent container must not have overflow-hidden.

API Key
This key is shown once and cannot be recovered.
Checksum
SHA-256 hash of the processed file.

Tabs

Bottom-border tab navigation for switching content within a page section. Active tab gets a colored bottom border and bold text. Content panels use x-show.

General settings content. Name, description, and basic configuration.

Permission settings content. Role assignments and access controls.

Advanced settings content. API keys, webhooks, and danger zone.

Theme Toggle

Sun/moon icon button that switches between dark and light themes. Toggles the dark class on <html>. When all markup uses semantic classes, switching themes only changes the @apply definitions — no markup changes needed. The toggle in the page header above is a live demo.

How it works: Tailwind's darkMode: "class" config enables the dark: prefix. Semantic classes define both variants (e.g. .card uses bg-white dark:bg-slate-900). The toggle calls document.documentElement.classList.toggle("dark").

Layout Patterns

App-level layout components for sidebars, split panes, and multi-step flows. These are more structural than the widget patterns above.

Collapsible Sidebar

Navigation sidebar that transitions between full-width (with labels) and narrow icon-only mode. Common in desktop apps (VS Code, Discord, Slack). The width animates via CSS transition. Click the logo/icon area to toggle. In collapsed mode, items show as icon-only buttons with tooltips (via title attribute). Badge counts show as small indicators in collapsed mode.

My App

List/Detail Split Pane

Common layout for browsing a collection: a scrollable list on the left and a content/reading pane on the right. Click an item in the list to see its details. When nothing is selected, the right pane shows a placeholder. The key CSS challenge is scroll containment — the list and detail pane scroll independently. Use min-h-0 on flex containers and overflow-y-auto on the scrollable areas. For a pinned header in the detail pane (header stays fixed while body scrolls), make the detail pane a flex column with the header as shrink-0 and the body as flex-1 overflow-y-auto.

Select an item to view

Stepper / Multi-Step Flow

Step progress indicator with numbered dots and connecting lines. Use inside modals or full-page wizards for setup flows, onboarding, or any multi-step process. Steps are numbered with dots that fill in as you progress. The content area switches based on the active step. Back/Next navigation in the footer, with the final step showing a different action button.

Step 1: Enter name, description, and basic details.

Step 2: Configure settings and options.

Step 3: Review and confirm your choices.
